subsection.title: Purpose
subsection.p: This document describes the complete system architecture, design decisions, and implementation plan for the DecSync Community Platform — a collaborative learning space built specifically for members of the DecSync batch. The platform enables resource sharing, technical blogging, community discussions, and DSA practice in a single unified interface.

principle.name: Zero cost during development
principle.description: Every tool, service, and platform used must have a free tier that comfortably supports a community of 50 to 300 students without requiring a credit card or trial account.

principle.name: Simplicity first
principle.description: The architecture starts as a well-organised monolith. Complexity is introduced only when genuinely needed.

principle.name: No self-hosted file storage
principle.description: All files such as PDFs, notes, and videos are stored on user-managed Google Drive or YouTube. The platform stores only metadata and URLs.

principle.name: Incrementally extensible
principle.description: Features are built in phases. The schema and API are designed so that later phases plug in cleanly without breaking existing work.

subsection.title: Scope
subsection.p: The platform covers the following functional areas:

scope_items.item: User registration, login, and profile management
Community forum discussions with nested comments
DSA problem lists with external links

section.title: Technology Stack
section.p: Every item in this stack is free to use for a project of this scale. No credit card, no trial, no hidden limits that would affect a community of 50 to 200 students.

entry.reason: SSR, file-based routing, API routes built in
500MB free, built-in auth, realtime, and RLS
Email and password, email verification, JWT sessions
Open source, Markdown and code block support
Built into Supabase, no extra infrastructure needed
100GB bandwidth per month, no credit card needed
3 emails per hour, sufficient for a small community

subsection.title: Architecture Style
subsection.p: The platform uses a monolithic Next.js application with a clear internal separation of concerns. The frontend and the backend API routes live in the same codebase and are deployed together on Vercel. The database and authentication are fully managed by Supabase. This avoids the operational overhead of running and maintaining separate services while still keeping the codebase organised enough to extract services later if the platform grows significantly.

steps.step: User opens a page, for example /resources
Next.js server component runs on Vercel serverless function
Server component calls Supabase directly using the service key
Supabase executes the query against PostgreSQL and applies RLS policies
Data is returned to the server component, which renders HTML
HTML is sent to the browser and the page appears immediately via SSR
React hydrates the page, making it interactive
Subsequent interactions such as votes and comments hit /api/* routes client-side
API routes validate the JWT from the user's cookie
Validated requests are executed against Supabase and results returned

subsection.title: Overview
subsection.p: Authentication is handled entirely by Supabase Auth using email and password. There is no Google Sign-In or any other OAuth provider. Users register with their name, email, password, and batch. Email verification is required before accessing the platform.

pages.page: Registration form accepting name, email, password, and batch
Login form accepting email and password
Form to request a password reset email
New password form reached via emailed link

subsection.title: Sign Up Flow
subsection.diagram: +------------------+ | Signup Form | | | | Name | | Email | | Password | | Batch | +--------+---------+ | | Submit v +--------+---------+ | Frontend | | Validation | <-- Zod schema: required fields, min 8 char password, | (client-side) | valid email format +--------+---------+ | | POST /api/auth/signup v +--------+---------+ | API Route | | Validation | <-- Server-side Zod validation (never trust client only) +--------+---------+ | v +--------+-----------------------------+ | supabase.auth.signUp({ | | email, | | password, | | options: { | | data: { name, batch } | | } | | }) | +--------+-----------------------------+ | | Supabase creates auth.users record | Supabase sends verification email automatically | DB trigger fires and inserts row into public.users v +--------+---------+ | Response to | | Browser: | | "Check your | | email to verify | | your account" | +--------+---------+ | | User clicks verification link in email v +--------+---------+ | Supabase marks | | email verified | | Session begins | | Redirect to / | +------------------+

subsection.title: Password Reset Flow
subsection.diagram: User clicks "Forgot Password" | v +--------+---------+ | Enter Email | +--------+---------+ | v +--------+----------------------------------------+ | supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail(email, | | { redirectTo: '/auth/reset' }) | +-------------------------------------------------+ | | Supabase sends reset link to email v User clicks link in email and lands on /auth/reset | v +--------+---------+ | Enter new | | password | +--------+---------+ | v +--------+---------------------------------------+ | supabase.auth.updateUser({ password: newPwd })| +-------------------------------------------------+ | v Redirect to /auth/login with success message

subsection.title: Session Management
subsection.p: The @supabase/ssr package is used to correctly handle Supabase sessions in Next.js. Tokens are stored in cookies and are available in both server components and client components. The access token expires every hour and is silently refreshed using the refresh token, which is valid for 7 days.

subsection.title: Route Protection Middleware
subsection.p: middleware.ts runs before every request on Vercel.

logic.rule: If no session and route is protected, redirect to /auth/login with next parameter
If session exists but role is not admin and route starts with /admin, redirect to /403

subsection.title: Database Trigger for New Users
subsection.p: When Supabase creates a new auth user, this trigger automatically creates the corresponding public profile row in public.users.

section.title: Database Schema
section.p: All tables live in Supabase's PostgreSQL instance. Row-Level Security is enabled on every table. Foreign key relationships are enforced at the database level.

subsection.title: Entity Relationship Overview
subsection.diagram:
users | +--< resources (one user uploads many resources) | +--< blog_posts (one user writes many blogs) | +--< forum_threads (one user creates many threads) | +--< forum_comments (one user writes many comments) | | | +--< forum_comments (self-referencing for nesting) | +--< votes (one user casts many votes) | +--< notifications (one user receives many notifications) | +--< achievements (one user earns many achievements) | +--< reports (one user files many reports)
forum_threads | +--< forum_comments (one thread has many comments)
announcements (standalone, only admins and mods create) dsa_problems (standalone, curated by admins) dsa_progress (user and problem join table, tracks completion)

column.name: role
column.constraints: DEFAULT student, CHECK IN (student, moderator, admin, banned)

column.name: author_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

column.name: category
column.constraints: CHECK IN (notes, pdf, placement, dsa, development, system_design, interview_exp, competitive, other)

column.name: author_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

column.name: author_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

column.name: category
column.constraints: CHECK IN (dsa, development, placements, projects, college, research, general)

column.name: parent_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES forum_comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, NULLABLE (null means top-level)

column.name: author_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

constraints.unique_constraint: UNIQUE on (user_id, target_id, target_type) — one vote per user per item
UNIQUE on (user_id, badge_key) — each badge earned only once

column.name: reporter_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

column.name: status
column.constraints: DEFAULT pending, CHECK IN (pending, resolved, dismissed)

column.name: author_id
column.constraints: REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

column.name: type
column.constraints: CHECK IN (placement, internship, hackathon, event, deadline, general)

column.name: platform
column.constraints: CHECK IN (leetcode, codeforces, codechef, gfg, other)

section.title: API Design
section.p: All API routes live under /api/ in the Next.js project. Every route validates the incoming request body using Zod. Routes that modify data require a valid session JWT extracted from the cookie by the Supabase SSR client.

subsection.title: Response Format

route.response: message: Reset email sent if account exists
user object with id, name, email, role, reputation

route.query_params: category, semester, subject, tags, sort (newest or popular), page, limit
route.response: paginated resource list with total count

route.body: title, description, category, semester, subject, tags, external_url, thumbnail_url
title, description, tags and other editable fields

route.query_params: tags, author, status, sort, page, limit
route.auth: none for published, required for own drafts

route.response: post with author, increments view count

route.query_params: category, sort (hot, new, top), page, limit

route.response: thread with nested comment tree, increments view count

route.auth: owner only, only if thread is not locked

route.response: success true, sets is_deleted true and replaces content with [deleted]
public profile with stats and achievements

route.query_params: q (required), type (all, resource, blog, forum, user), page, limit
route.response: grouped results for resources, blogs, forums, and users with total count
success true, sets role to banned without deleting user

subsection.title: Project Structure
subsection.diagram: decsync/ | +-- app/ Next.js App Router pages | | | +-- page.tsx Home / Dashboard | +-- layout.tsx Root layout (navbar, footer) | | | +-- auth/ | | +-- signup/page.tsx | | +-- login/page.tsx | | +-- verify/page.tsx | | +-- forgot/page.tsx | | +-- reset/page.tsx | | | +-- resources/ | | +-- page.tsx Resource listing | | +-- upload/page.tsx Upload form | | +-- [id]/page.tsx Resource detail | | | +-- blogs/ | | +-- page.tsx Blog listing | | +-- write/page.tsx Blog editor | | +-- [slug]/page.tsx Blog reader | | | +-- forums/ | | +-- page.tsx Thread listing | | +-- new/page.tsx Create thread | | +-- [id]/page.tsx Thread with comments | | | +-- profile/ | | +-- [id]/page.tsx Public profile view | | +-- edit/page.tsx Edit own profile | | | +-- search/page.tsx | +-- announcements/page.tsx | +-- dsa/page.tsx | +-- admin/page.tsx | +-- 403/page.tsx | +-- api/ API route handlers | +-- auth/ | +-- resources/ | +-- blogs/ | +-- forums/ | +-- users/ | +-- search/ | +-- notifications/ | +-- admin/ | +-- components/ | | | +-- layout/ | | +-- Navbar.tsx | | +-- Sidebar.tsx | | +-- Footer.tsx | | | +-- auth/ | | +-- SignupForm.tsx | | +-- LoginForm.tsx | | +-- ForgotForm.tsx | | +-- ResetForm.tsx | | | +-- resources/ | | +-- ResourceCard.tsx | | +-- ResourceGrid.tsx | | +-- ResourceFilter.tsx | | +-- ResourceUploadForm.tsx | | | +-- blogs/ | | +-- BlogCard.tsx | | +-- BlogEditor.tsx | | +-- BlogViewer.tsx | | | +-- forums/ | | +-- ThreadCard.tsx | | +-- CommentTree.tsx | | +-- CommentItem.tsx | | +-- CommentForm.tsx | | | +-- profile/ | | +-- ProfileHeader.tsx | | +-- AchievementBadge.tsx | | +-- ContributionStats.tsx | | +-- ReputationBadge.tsx | | | +-- shared/ | +-- VoteButton.tsx | +-- SearchBar.tsx | +-- FilterPanel.tsx | +-- MarkdownRenderer.tsx | +-- Avatar.tsx | +-- Tag.tsx | +-- Modal.tsx | +-- Toast.tsx | +-- Skeleton.tsx | +-- NotificationBell.tsx | +-- NotificationPanel.tsx | +-- ReportButton.tsx | +-- AnnouncementBanner.tsx | +-- lib/ | +-- supabase/ | | +-- server.ts | | +-- client.ts | +-- validations/ | +-- reputation.ts | +-- achievements.ts | +-- search.ts | +-- slugify.ts | +-- middleware.ts +-- tailwind.config.ts +-- next.config.ts

strategy.name: Auth State
strategy.description: Global and managed via React Context. The Supabase session is read once on app load and stored in a context that all components can access. This tells every component whether the user is logged in and what their role is.

strategy.name: Server Data
strategy.description: Managed by TanStack Query (React Query). It handles caching, background refetching, pagination, and optimistic updates. Every API call goes through a custom hook that wraps a React Query query or mutation.

hooks.hook: useVote(targetId, type) — mutation for vote endpoints
useThread(id) — fetches thread with comment tree
useNotifications() — fetches and subscribes via Supabase Realtime

subsection.title: Full-Text Search
subsection.p: A search_vector column of type TSVECTOR is maintained on the resources, blog_posts, and forum_threads tables. A database trigger updates this column automatically on insert or update. A GIN index on each search_vector column ensures fast query performance.
Global search query across all content types:
subsection.code:
SELECT 'blog' AS type, id, title, slug AS ref FROM blog_posts WHERE search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $1) AND status = 'published'
UNION ALL
SELECT 'resource' AS type, id, title, id::text AS ref FROM resources WHERE search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $1)
UNION ALL
SELECT 'forum' AS type, id, title, id::text AS ref FROM forum_threads WHERE search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $1)
ORDER BY type;

subsection.title: Nested Forum Comments
subsection.p: Comments support two levels of nesting: a top-level comment and one level of replies. The full tree is fetched in a single recursive CTE query and assembled into a visual hierarchy by the CommentTree component.

achievement.condition: User publishes their first blog post
User creates their first forum thread
User has activity on 7 consecutive days
User has activity on 30 consecutive days
User marks 10 DSA problems as solved
User marks 50 DSA problems as solved

subsection.title: Blog Editor
subsection.p: The blog editor uses TipTap with the following extensions configured. Content is stored as sanitized HTML. DOMPurify is applied on the server before saving and on the client before rendering to prevent stored XSS attacks. Slugs are generated from the blog title at creation time and de-duplicated with a numeric suffix if needed.

extensions.extension: StarterKit — bold, italic, headings, lists, blockquote, horizontal rule
CodeBlockLowlight — code blocks with Shiki syntax highlighting
CharacterCount — shows word and character count

subsection.title: Row-Level Security
subsection.p: RLS is enabled on every table. Supabase evaluates these policies at the database level so that even if an API route has a bug, the database itself will reject unauthorised reads or writes.

subsection.title: Input Validation
subsection.p: Every API route validates its input using a Zod schema before processing anything. Validation runs on the server regardless of what the client sends.
subsection.code: const createResourceSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(3).max(200), description: z.string().max(2000).optional(), category: z.enum(['notes','pdf','placement','dsa', 'development','system_design', 'interview_exp','competitive','other']), semester: z.number().int().min(1).max(8).optional(), subject: z.string().max(100).optional(), tags: z.array(z.string().max(30)).max(10).default([]), external_url: z.string().url(), });

subsection.title: Content Sanitization
subsection.p: All user-generated HTML from the blog editor and forum posts is sanitized using DOMPurify before being stored in the database and again before being rendered in the browser. This prevents stored XSS attacks regardless of what the editor produces.

week.title: Project Setup and Deployment Pipeline

tasks.task: Initialise Next.js project with TypeScript and App Router
Configure Tailwind CSS and base design tokens
Set up Supabase project including database, auth, and RLS
Create all database tables and triggers from the schema
Connect Next.js to Supabase using @supabase/ssr
Configure Vercel deployment with GitHub CI/CD
Set up ESLint, Prettier, and Husky pre-commit hooks
Create the base layout skeleton: Navbar, Sidebar, Footer

week.deliverable: Deployed skeleton at a Vercel URL with database connected

week.title: Authentication System

tasks.task: Build signup page and form accepting name, email, password, and batch
Build login page and form
Build verify, forgot, and reset password pages
Implement route protection middleware
Add session-aware navbar showing name and logout when signed in
Implement the database trigger for auto-creating public.users
Write Zod validation schemas for all auth inputs
Test the full auth flow end-to-end

week.deliverable: Users can register, verify email, log in, log out, and reset their password

week.title: Resources Section

tasks.task: Build resource listing page with category and sort filters
Build resource detail page
Build resource upload form with metadata and external URL field
Implement tag filtering with GIN-indexed queries
Implement upvoting using the votes table and UI button
Add basic PostgreSQL full-text search on resources
Award reputation points on resource upload and upvote events

week.deliverable: Full resource upload and discovery experience working end-to-end

week.title: Blog Section

tasks.task: Integrate TipTap editor with code block and Shiki syntax highlighting
Build blog editor page with write and draft saving
Build blog listing page with tag filters
Build blog reader page with sanitized HTML rendering
Implement draft versus published status flow
Auto-generate slugs from title with de-duplication
Add upvoting on blog posts
Add full-text search on blog posts

week.deliverable: Students can write, save drafts, publish, and read blogs

week.title: Forum System

tasks.task: Build thread listing page with category filter and sort options
Build thread detail page with full nested comment tree
Build create thread page
Implement recursive comment fetching using CTE query
Build comment reply UI supporting two levels of nesting
Implement upvoting on threads and comments
Add lock and pin controls for moderators
Add full-text search on forum threads

week.deliverable: Full working forum with nested discussions

week.title: Search and Notifications

tasks.task: Build global search page combining resources, blogs, and forums
Implement unified search query using UNION ALL
Build notification bell component and dropdown panel
Set up Supabase Realtime subscription for unread notification count
Implement notification insertion on reply and mention events
Parse @mentions in forum content and trigger notifications

week.deliverable: Global search works. Users receive live notifications.

week.title: User Profiles and Reputation

tasks.task: Build public profile page showing bio, stats, achievements, and activity
Build edit profile page for name, bio, avatar URL, and batch
Implement reputation point tracking across all actions
Display reputation level and badge on profile and navbar
Implement achievement unlock logic with badge UI
Build contribution stats section showing blog, resource, and thread counts

week.deliverable: Full user profile with reputation and achievements visible

week.title: Moderation and Admin

tasks.task: Build admin dashboard page protected by role check
Build report queue view showing pending reports
Implement report button on all content types
Implement resolve and dismiss actions on reports
Implement ban user action that sets role to banned
Build announcement creation form
Build announcements listing page
Add announcement banner on home page for active announcements

week.deliverable: Admins and moderators can moderate content and post announcements

week.title: DSA Section

tasks.task: Build DSA problem listing page with filter by topic, difficulty, and platform
Implement mark as attempted and solved using dsa_progress table
Display user progress inline on the listing page
Highlight weekly challenge problems
Build admin form to add and edit DSA problems

week.deliverable: Structured DSA practice section with progress tracking

week.title: Leaderboards and Gamification

tasks.task: Build reputation leaderboard showing top contributors this week and all time
Build DSA leaderboard showing most problems solved
Implement activity streak tracking based on daily actions
Display streak count on profile
Polish achievement badge gallery on profile page

week.deliverable: Leaderboards live. Streaks and gamification fully visible.

week.title: Discovery and Trending

tasks.task: Build trending section on home dashboard showing most upvoted content this week
Add related posts on blog and forum pages using shared tag overlap
Add personalized feed on home page based on user's most-used tags
Improve filter UX across all listing pages

week.deliverable: Home dashboard shows meaningful personalized and trending content

week.title: Testing and Hardening

tasks.task: Write end-to-end tests for core flows using Playwright
Audit all database queries for performance and add missing indexes
Run Lighthouse audit on key pages and fix performance issues
Verify all RLS policies correctly block unauthorised access
Review and tighten rate limiting rules
Write contributor documentation including README, setup guide, and DB schema docs

week.deliverable: Stable, tested, documented platform ready for community use

feature.name: Integrated Online Judge
feature.notes: Use Judge0 open source, self-hostable on Oracle Cloud free VPS, for in-platform code submission

feature.name: AI Search
feature.notes: Supabase pgvector for semantic search using free embedding APIs

feature.name: Recommendation Engine
feature.notes: Collaborative filtering on resource and blog interactions

feature.name: Placement Dashboard
feature.notes: Company-wise resource grouping and offer tracker

feature.name: Email Digest
feature.notes: Weekly summary of top content, requires email provider upgrade beyond Supabase free tier

limit.impact: Sufficient for text content. Files live on external links only.
More than enough for this community.
Fine for normal signups. Could queue during a large onboarding rush.
Sufficient. No video or file hosting on the platform.
More than enough for regular deployments.

subsection.title: When to Upgrade
subsection.p: The platform will not need paid services until one of the following conditions is met: the platform expands beyond the DecSync batch to the entire college or multiple batches with 500 or more active users; direct file hosting becomes necessary instead of external Drive links; or email digest features require more than 3 emails per hour consistently. At that point only two upgrades are needed: Supabase Pro at 25 USD per month and Vercel Pro at 20 USD per month. Everything else in the stack remains free regardless of scale.
